steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The full sentence is: 'San Juan High School and Grand County High School are two of several Utah ______ enormous geoglyph of the letters SJ overlooks San Juan High, while a geoglyph of the letter G overlooks Grand County High.' The blank must complete 'Utah ______' and then the rest must form a grammatical continuation. The second independent clause ('An enormous geoglyph...') is a separate sentence.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'schools that have their own hillside geoglyphs. An': Ends the first sentence cleanly with a restrictive relative clause and starts a new sentence with 'An.' Both sentences are complete and grammatical. ✓
  • Choice B — 'schools, that have their own hillside geoglyphs and an': The comma before 'that' incorrectly punctuates a restrictive relative clause, and connecting with 'and an' fuses two unrelated clauses into one run-on without a main verb for the second. ✗
  • Choice C — 'schools that have their own hillside geoglyphs, an': The comma after 'geoglyphs' attempts to continue with 'an enormous geoglyph,' creating a comma splice — two independent clauses joined only by a comma. ✗
  • Choice D — 'schools, that have their own hillside geoglyphs, and an': Both commas around 'that have their own hillside geoglyphs' incorrectly set off a restrictive clause as nonrestrictive, and 'and an' still produces a run-on. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
